metadata.title: Defense leg - primes + defense-tech x the rare-earth chokepoint
metadata.provenance: Web-verified (CSIS, Modern War Institute/West Point, GAO/CRS-cited reporting, TechCrunch/CNBC, DoD FY26 budget, Arnold/Dura Magnetics). Per-unit REE figures are widely-cited estimates (GAO/CRS lineage); treat as order-of-magnitude.

rare_earth_dependency.note: ~900 lbs REE/jet; SmCo magnets for high-temp (missile nose cones); Lockheed = largest US samarium user. Block 4 upgrade slipped 2026->2031, +$6B.
per-missile small but volume is huge

china_control.controls: Apr 2025: export licenses required on 7 heavy REEs (Sm, Gd, Tb, Dy, Lu, Sc, Y) + magnets. Dec 1 2025: entities affiliated with FOREIGN MILITARIES (incl. US) largely DENIED licenses; military-use requests auto-rejected. Nov 7 2025 Trump-Xi deal paused broad controls 1yr but RETAINED the military-supply chokehold.

domestic_response.fact: MP Materials (DoD ~15% equity + $110/kg NdPr floor + $150M loan + 10yr offtake) and USA Rare Earth not at full magnet scale until 2027-2028; samarium resilience 3-7 yrs; full mine-to-magnet 7-12+ yrs. DoD using DPA Title III + Office of Strategic Capital 'equity-like investments + offtake' (the state-as-vendor-financier model).

defense_tech.name: Anduril
defense_tech.valuation: $30.5B (Jun-2025 Series G, Founders Fund led, $1B largest-ever FF check, 8x oversubscribed) -> $61B (May-2026 Series H, Thrive + a16z); revenue ~$2.2B 2025 (doubled). Army IVAS reassigned from Microsoft ($22B program, Feb 2026); Army enterprise contract consolidating 120 procurement actions (Mar 2026).

defense_tech.name: Palantir
defense_tech.valuation: ~$350B market cap (approx, 2025-26); Army Maven Smart System; ~$10B Army enterprise agreement.

dod_budget.detail: $838.7B appropriations + $150B One Big Beautiful Bill; FY27 request ~$1,500B.

proven_results.chokepoint: Z3: REE-independent flagship production is UNSAT for 2026 (supply ~1,700 kg vs ~5,550 kg/yr demand); earliest feasible year 2028; 2026-27 REQUIRES China. Binding constraint = mine-to-magnet years, not dollars.
proven_results.debasement: DoD budget +3.7x nominal (1998 $270B -> 2026 $1T) but -75% in gold (918M -> 233M oz). The $1T military buys ~25% of the gold the 1998 military did.
proven_results.allied_mincut: Max-flow/min-cut (data/allied_mincut.json): allies+domestic cannot meet demand without China until ~2028; the MIN-CUT is consistently midstream SEPARATION/magnet-making, NOT mining (allied mining ~11,000 kg/yr >> 5,550 demand). Re-shoring mines does not fix the gap; only midstream processing does.
